checks.finding: Every AC-NNN referenced in tasks.md (AC-001..035, AC-039..048) resolves to a matching row in acceptance-tests.md; no dangling ID found.

All five Blockers fields (T-001: None; T-002: T-001; T-003: T-001, T-002; T-004: T-001, T-002, T-003; T-005: T-001, T-002, T-003, T-004) are literal None or comma-separated T-\d{3} lists; no range notation, no prose in the field value. blockers_format_valid=true, matching precheck-result.json.

REQ-001..REQ-008 each appear in at least one task's Requirements: field (REQ-001 T-001; REQ-002 T-002; REQ-003 T-003; REQ-004/REQ-005 T-004; REQ-006/REQ-008 shared across T-001..T-005; REQ-007 T-001).

AC-001..048 (excluding Global AC-036/037/038) all map to a task per traceability.md's Acceptance Mapping table. AC-036/037/038 are documented in traceability.md's 'Global Acceptance Criteria' section as already satisfied by this feature's own frozen spec-phase registration commit -- a stated, reasoned deferral, not an uncovered AC.
Every task (T-001..T-005) carries a non-empty Requirements: field citing at least one REQ-NNN; no orphan task.

Every task in this feature (whether or not its title matches the test/spec/verify heuristic) references multiple AC-NNN identifiers in its Requirements/Done When fields; no test task is orphaned from acceptance criteria.
Round 2 (subsequent-round rule applied, pre-implementation): all five tasks carry Approval: Draft (not Approved) and Status: Planned (a valid enum member of Planned/In Progress/Blocked).

T-001 Risk: high / Required Workflow: tdd; T-002 Risk: high / Required Workflow: tdd (revised this round per reviewer-b's round-1 RISK-APPROPRIATE finding -- Risk Rationale now cites risk-classification-policy.md's 'public API contracts' high-tier trigger directly and explicitly disclaims the prior self-certified narrowing the policy forbids); T-003 high/tdd; T-004 high/tdd; T-005 medium/acceptance-first. Every pairing matches risk-gate-matrix.md's tier-to-workflow mapping; precheck-result.json workflow_match_precheck=PASS corroborates independently.

No AC-NNN is claimed as the primary test target by two tasks without differentiation. AC-035 is shared across all five tasks' Requirements fields by explicit design (REQ-008's per-task CHANGELOG-bullet + version-mutation-grep obligation); traceability.md's Acceptance Mapping table documents this as 'each task's own CHANGELOG bullet + own no-version-bump grep self-check' -- a distinct per-task instantiation, not duplicate testing of one assertion.

Every task ID referenced in a Blockers field (T-001 in T-002/T-003/T-004/T-005; T-002 in T-003/T-004/T-005; T-003 in T-004/T-005; T-004 in T-005) exists in dependency-graph.json's nodes array [T-001..T-005].

BLOCKERS-FORMAT is PASS, so this check ran. dependency-graph.json's edges (T-002->T-001, T-003->T-001, T-003->T-002, T-004->T-001, T-004->T-002, T-004->T-003, T-005->T-001, T-005->T-002, T-005->T-003, T-005->T-004) form a strict DAG -- every edge points to a strictly lower-numbered task, so no path can revisit a node. No cycle.

T-001..T-004's 'and' clauses tie schema/validator authorship to the directly-associated test suite(s) for that same artifact (exception 1). T-005 bundles the vendored-copy drift-gate extension and the cross-script parity suite; its own 'Note on scope breadth (TASK-SIZE)' explains both are one coherent, non-decomposable verification concern over the four already-implemented scripts (the parity suite's AC-032 fixtures are the mechanism exercising the vendored copies the drift gate protects), not two distinct feature concerns.

Grep across all five Done When lists found no forbidden verb/phrase (ensure, consider, unmeasured 'update X', 'verify is correct', 'works correctly', unattributed 'review X'/'confirm X'). Every Done When item names a concrete test ID plus an exit-0 shell command (e.g. 'sh tests/facet-manifest-schema.tests.sh exits 0'), a recorded log/record file (verification/T-00N/{red,green}-sh.log, metaschema-conformance.md, vendor-check.log), or a specific governance artifact (CHANGELOG.md bullet, MANIFEST.sha256 entry). No item requires editing a review-frozen artifact (design.md/tasks.md/traceability.md).

All five task IDs (T-001..T-005) appear in traceability.md's Task Mapping table; every requirement ID in traceability.md (REQ-001..008) exists in requirements.md. Each requirement row carries valid canonical <layer>-spec.md#<section> anchors verified against actual headings/content in security-spec.md (#trust-boundaries, #stride-analysis, #security-tests, #sbom-and-supply-chain) and infra-spec.md (#deployment-topology, #cicd-sequence, #data-residency-and-retention), or a reasoned N/A -- cross-layer only: <reason> value (REQ-008).
